findings.description: README claims '300+ passing unit tests' but actual count is 559 unit tests (only +259, not +300 beyond baseline). README claims '16 passing integration tests' but no integration test directory or framework found. Toolchain pinned to 'Rust 1.92.0' but Cargo.toml declares 'rust-version = 1.85'. README claims 'skill-registry' as core component but skills CLI is orphaned from actual skill ecosystem.
findings.deferral_reason: Documentation drift is lower priority than active functionality issues; README may be aspirational architecture documentation vs. current state

findings.lines: dynamic_intrinsics.rs, epistemic.rs, intent.rs, json_processor.rs, routing_engine.rs, session_tracker.rs, stats.rs, strategy.rs
findings.description: Eight modules declared in mod.rs (80 public modules) but 30 modules not re-exported via lib.rs (only 24 exported). dynamic_intrinsics.rs is orphaned (module exists, not used by CLI, not tested in unit test runs—only intrinsic_is_prime function present with no call sites). epistemic.rs (13 functions exported, 13 tests—never called). intent.rs (classify_intent function, 5 tests—never called). json_processor.rs (12 public functions, 40+ tests—never called). routing_engine.rs (module exists, never wired to CLI). session_tracker.rs (never called). stats.rs (4 public functions—tests pass but module never invoked). strategy.rs (module exists, zero exports).
findings.deferral_reason: These modules may be foundational for future CLI expansion or MCP tools; removing them would require confirmation of intent. However, the lack of CLI wiring or test coverage suggests they are incomplete.

findings.description: Two handler files exist (simple.rs, compress.rs) that are listed in handlers/mod.rs but never called by any CLI action. simple.rs defines functions handle_variance, handle_version, handle_fuzzy, handle_levenshtein, handle_build, handle_chain, handle_evolve, handle_sha256 but there is no wiring in main.rs or actions.rs that calls these functions. These are orphaned handler implementations.
findings.deferral_reason: Handlers may be planned but not yet wired; simple.rs could be a catch-all module for utility commands. Confirm intent before removal.

findings.description: forge feature flag declared (line 66: `forge = []`) but dependency never added (line 60: `# forge-spec not yet available — uncomment when rust-forge is populated`). The forge handler file exists (340 lines) but depends on `use forge_spec::*` which is never imported. forge-spec crate does not exist in the workspace. Handler code cannot compile unless feature is explicitly disabled—this is dead code protected by cfg(feature = "forge").
findings.deferral_reason: Intentional stub awaiting rust-forge crate population; acceptable dead code behind feature gate. Document the deadline for activation or remove if rust-forge is never populated.

findings.description: rsk-mcp crate fully implemented (main.rs, lib.rs, server.rs with 16 MCP tools defined) but NOT registered in ~/.claude.json MCP server config. Crate compiles cleanly, exposes `rsk_health`, `mcg_run`, `mcg_test`, `mcg_test_all`, `mcg_chain`, `mcg_chain_test`, `mcg_list`, `mcg_info`, `mcg_coverage`, `stats_chi_square`, `stats_t_test`, `stats_proportion_test`, `stats_correlation`, `decision_tree_run`, `graph_topsort`, `graph_parallel_levels` but these tools are unreachable to Claude Code sessions. MCP server binary exists but is never invoked.
findings.deferral_reason: Requires MCP server spawning configuration; this is a deployment/registration issue, not a code defect. rsk-mcp is fully functional but dormant.

findings.description: 389 micrograms are not referenced by any chain definition. Of 460 total micrograms, only 71 are called by chain definitions in rsk/chains/. Examples: ab-charge-imbalance, ab-conservation-violation, ab-dopamine-spike, abstract-pv-relevance, action-irreversibility-gate, adverse-reaction-extractor, adr-classifier, agent-capability-gate, etc. These micrograms pass their own self-tests (5113 tests passed, 0 failed) but are never invoked as part of any workflow. Classification: either (a) foundational utilities awaiting chain composition, (b) abandoned experiments, or (c) unfinished CLI integration.
findings.deferral_reason: Orphaned micrograms may be intentionally modular building blocks not yet composed into chains. However, MEMORY.md notes 460 micrograms as existing count; only 71 are discoverable via chains. Requires clarification on which are dead vs. intentional orphans.

findings.description: Chain test warnings show '118 passed' across 33 chains but 1 parse error: `narrative-safe-extract.yaml: steps[0]: invalid type: map, expected a string`. Chain YAML schema expects `steps` to be a list of strings (microgram names) but at least one chain definition has a structured object instead of a string. Multiple chains also emit warnings: 'No primitive_signature on [microgram]' for ~70+ distinct micrograms in chains, indicating schema validation is lenient (warnings, not errors). Signature contract mismatch.
findings.deferral_reason: Chain parser is tolerant (continues despite warnings). Primitive_signature absence is a documentation gap, not a runtime failure. However, the parse error in narrative-safe-extract.yaml indicates a real schema inconsistency.

findings.description: Microgram self-testing framework reports '460 micrograms, 5113 passed, 0 failed' but does NOT distinguish between actual assertion failures and silently passing test cases. Test runner emits JSON summary but does not log individual assertion failures inline. If a microgram's test block has only comments and no actual assertions, it would report as 'passed'. Cannot verify test quality without examining individual YAML test blocks.
findings.deferral_reason: Test runner may be correct; YAML test structure needs spot-check. Not theater if tests have real assertions; conservative classification pending code review.

findings.description: README lists 'rust-forge' as component under Project Structure (line 12: '**rust-forge**: Pipeline compilation framework for high-throughput data processing') but rsk-forge crate does NOT exist in the workspace. rust-forge is only a reference in Cargo.toml comments (line 12: '# rust-forge crates not yet populated'). It is listed as a workspace member (line 5: members = ["rsk", "rsk-mcp"]) but commented out (line 8). README describes a product that is not yet built.
findings.deferral_reason: Intentional forward documentation; rust-forge is planned but not populated. Update README to clarify 'planned' vs. 'implemented'.

findings.description: Chain validator emits 'No primitive_signature on [microgram]' warnings for 70+ micrograms but does not fail validation. Chains with missing signatures still execute and pass their tests. Validator is cosmetic—it reports schema violations but does not enforce them. Signature contract should either be mandatory (error on mismatch) or advisory (lowercase warning, update docs).
findings.deferral_reason: Requires deeper inspection of validator logic; warnings suggest intentional leniency, but the number of warnings (70+) suggests systematic drift rather than edge cases.

findings.description: Cargo.toml declares version = '0.5.1' (line 3) but README references it as part of a 'Gemini Rust Workspace' with no version number clarity. No changelog or version history documentation. Version bump from 0.1.0 to 0.5.1 suggests 4 minor releases, but CLAUDE.md or changelog file does not exist to document what changed.
findings.deferral_reason: Version documentation is nice-to-have; does not affect runtime. Low priority.

findings.description: Feature 'python' declared (line 64: `python = ["pyo3"]`) and pyo3 module exists (python_bindings.rs, 49 lines) but disabled by default (`default = []`). Python feature is not tested in standard test run and not documented in README. CLI has no python subcommand. Feature exists but is orphaned from the project's primary use case (MCP exposure via rsk-mcp).
findings.deferral_reason: Python bindings may be for future external consumption; not part of current roadmap. Acceptable dead code if explicitly planned for later.

findings.description: Microgram runtime accepts HashMap<String, Value> input but does not validate that all required input fields are present. Test scaffolding includes `test: input: {}` to validate empty input handling, but production micrograms in rsk/micrograms/ do not show explicit null-safety guards in their YAML definitions. If a microgram references a field `prr` and input omits it, runtime behavior depends on decision tree implementation (likely returns null/default).
findings.deferral_reason: Microgram framework appears to handle null gracefully; requires spot-check of a few real micrograms to confirm safety contracts.

CRITICAL: Documentation-Implementation Drift: README claims vs. actual state
Dead Code: 30 modules declared but not exported / orphaned from CLI
HIGH: Unwired Hooks: rsk-mcp fully built but not registered in Claude Code
State Schema Drift: 389/460 micrograms orphaned from chains
Schema Validation: Chain validator warns but does not enforce
MEDIUM: Echo Theater: Test framework lacks assertion visibility
Documentation Drift: rust-forge planned but not implemented
Feature Orphan: python feature disabled, unused
Schema Safety: Microgram input validation not verified

recommendations.action: Register rsk-mcp in ~/.claude.json to expose 16 tools to Claude Code sessions. Current state: fully implemented but dormant.
recommendations.rationale: rsk-mcp is production-ready; registration requires only config entry.

recommendations.action: Audit and document the 389 orphaned micrograms. Determine: (1) Are they foundational utilities awaiting chain composition? (2) Are they abandoned experiments? (3) Do they need CLI integration? Create a backlog if not in current roadmap.
recommendations.rationale: 389/460 micrograms are unreachable; this represents significant unmeasured state. MEMORY.md counts 460 as the inventory; unclear which are intentional vs. dead.

recommendations.action: Update README.md to reflect current state: 559 unit tests (not 300+), no integration tests (correct if none), Rust 1.85 (not 1.92.0), and clarify which components are implemented vs. planned (rust-forge, python bindings).
recommendations.rationale: Documentation drift causes misalignment on scope and capability.

recommendations.action: Review 30 undeclared modules (epistemic, intent, json_processor, routing_engine, session_tracker, strategy, etc.) and either: (a) wire them into CLI / MCP, (b) move them to a planned feature branch, or (c) delete them. Confirm intent before removal.
recommendations.rationale: Dead code increases maintenance burden; clarity on intent prevents regressions.

recommendations.action: Fix narrative-safe-extract.yaml parse error (steps[0]: invalid type: map, expected a string). Validate all chain YAML files conform to schema.
recommendations.rationale: 1 chain fails to parse; unclear impact on execution.

recommendations.action: Review chain validator: 70+ micrograms are missing `primitive_signature`. Either (a) add signatures to all micrograms, (b) make validator errors instead of warnings, or (c) remove signature validation if it's cosmetic.
recommendations.rationale: Warnings at scale suggest systematic drift, not edge cases.

recommendations.action: Document microgram input validation contract. Confirm that all micrograms handle missing/null inputs safely, or add pre-flight validation to the runtime.
recommendations.rationale: Low risk if framework handles nulls gracefully; confirmation eliminates latent failure modes.

verification_notes.test_execution: Unit tests: 559 passed, 0 failed. Microgram self-tests: 460 micrograms, 5113 tests passed, 0 failed. Chain tests: 33 chains passed, 1 parse error (narrative-safe-extract.yaml). Compilation: rsk and rsk-mcp both compile without warnings or errors in release mode.
verification_notes.scope: Full rsk-core workspace: 2 crates, 103 Rust source files, ~17.6k lines total. Scanned: lib.rs, mod.rs (all modules), Cargo.toml (features), CLI handlers, chain definitions, micrograms.
verification_notes.clean_scan_protocol: Zero findings from Class 1 (echo theater) or Class 7 (jq dialect). Clippy emits zero warnings. Tests pass uniformly. This is a FALSE CLEAN BILL — the orphaned modules, undiscovered tools, and schema drift are real findings masked by high test pass rates.
